what_it_does: A posted noticeboard note in which Tim replies to a prior item, arguing that the comment/edge machinery should be aimed at cards through the existing annotation layer and that the system should be assumed to be intentionally larger than it currently appears.
observations: YAML frontmatter follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, timestamps, and a commented_on link to item-7c7dbc35.
Body is a short point/comment tagged with [point].
It references 'cards', 'members feed and correct them', and 'the annotation layer that already exists'.
It states 'Literally nothing in any of this is in constant operation, it is all footholds.'
It advocates assuming 'more' and that the extra structure is 'intentionally more to probably build'.
standouts: Direct response to item-7c7dbc35 about pointing comment/edge machinery at cards.
Strong 'footholds' framing: current pieces are not continuously operating final mechanisms.
Explicit call to assume an intentionally larger design space.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema and board:// URI addressing.
Uses a [point] tag in the markdown body.
Links to the prior item via a commented_on relation.
concerns: Relies on undefined local terms such as 'cards', 'annotation layer', and 'comment/edge machinery' without explaining them in this file.
notes: Tim's note pushing back that the comment/edge machinery idea should assume a bigger, intentionally-built system where current artifacts are temporary footholds.
questions: What is the 'annotation layer that already exists'?
What is the 'comment/edge machinery' being discussed?
What did the referenced item-7c7dbc35 propose?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is labeled as a point/comment and its content is exactly a design-opinion reply to another noticeboard item.
intention_signals: To register a design stance: treat current mechanisms as footholds, assume more intentional structure will be built, and support routing comment/edge activity through the existing annotation layer rather than treating current machinery as complete.
novelty: The explicit 'footholds' framing that nothing in the current system is in constant operation and the whole is intentionally larger than visible.
contribution: Captures a thread-linked design opinion about how comment/edge machinery, cards, and an existing annotation layer should relate, plus a memorable framing that current artifacts are footholds for a larger system.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development replying to item-7c7dbc35, arguing that comment/edge machinery should target cards through the existing annotation layer and that the system is intentionally larger than it appears, with current pieces serving as footholds.
intention_for_embedding: To record Tim's stance that current implementation pieces are footholds for an intentionally larger system, not continuously operating final machinery.
